question: According to Hadith 1, to whom is sincerity (nasihah) owed?

options.B: To Allah, His Book, His Messenger, leaders and common folk

question: What does the word "An-Nasihah" mean in this hadith?
This Hadith emphasises that sincerity to Muslim leaders includes:

options.D: Following them regardless of Islamic principles

arabic_text: لَا يُؤْمِنُ أَحَدُكُمْ حَتَّى يُحِبَّ لِأَخِيهِ مَا يُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِهِ
english_translation: None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself
question: According to Hadith 2, what is a condition of complete faith?

options.B: Loving for your brother what you love for yourself
options.D: Giving all your wealth to charity

arabic_text: لَا يُؤْمِنُ أَحَدُكُمْ حَتَّى يُحِبَّ لِأَخِيهِ مَا يُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِهِ
english_translation: None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself
question: In which collection is this Hadith about loving for your brother found?

arabic_text: لَا يُؤْمِنُ أَحَدُكُمْ حَتَّى يُحِبَّ لِأَخِيهِ مَا يُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِهِ
english_translation: None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself
question: The principle in this Hadith primarily teaches:

options.A: Selfishness is part of human nature

english_translation: Whoever sees an evil, let him change it with his hand
question: According to Hadith 3, what is the weakest form of faith when addressing evil?

english_translation: Whoever sees an evil, let him change it with his hand
question: How many methods of changing evil are mentioned in Hadith 3?
According to Hadith 9, what does modesty (Al-Haya) always bring?
The Arabic word "Al-Haya" in Hadith 9 means:

arabic_text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَنْظُرُ إِلَى صُوَرِكُمْ وَأَمْوَالِكُمْ، وَلَكِنْ يَنْظُرُ إِلَى قُلُوبِكُمْ وَأَعْمَالِكُمْ
english_translation: Allah does not look at your appearance or your wealth, but He looks at your hearts and your actions
question: According to Hadith 11, what does Allah judge a person by?

arabic_text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَنْظُرُ إِلَى صُوَرِكُمْ وَأَمْوَالِكُمْ
english_translation: Allah does not look at your appearance or your wealth
question: What lesson does Hadith 11 teach about equality in Islam?

options.A: Rich people are preferred by Allah
options.B: All people are equal before Allah regardless of wealth
options.D: Social status determines one's rank in Islam
